title: adesmia retrofracta
intro: Adesmia retrofracta, also known as the "false lupine" or "Peruvian lupine," is a flowering plant native to South America, specifically in the Andean regions. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of alkaloids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Adesmia retrofracta include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water to create a tea, or by drying and grinding the plant material to make a powder for use in capsules or as a tincture.
